title: Ceropegia plicatiloba
taxonomy: Ceropegia plicatiloba belongs to the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes over 400 genera and more than 5,000 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, one of which is the Asclepiadoideae subfamily, to which Ceropegia plicatiloba also belongs. Ceropegia plicatiloba is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ceropegia, which is a group of succulent plants native to Africa. The classification of Ceropegia plicatiloba as a species within the Apocynaceae family provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
